session.tool_result:
18→When a plan becomes a PR, erk establishes a linkage that allows: 19→ 20→- `erk pr list` to show which plans have associated PRs 21→- PRs to automatically close their source plan when merged 22→- The 🔗 indicator to appear for auto-closing PRs 23→ 24→## How Linkages Are Created 25→ 26→### Via `erk pr submit` 27→ 28→The primary path for creating issue-PR linkages: 29→ 30→1. Creates branch from plan issue (e.g., `P123-feature-12-11-0948`) 31→2. Creates `.erk/impl-context/` folder with `ref.json` containing plan reference 32→3. Creates draft PR with `Closes #N` in **initial** body 33→4. GitHub registers the `CrossReferencedEvent` with `willCloseTarget: true` 34→ 35→**Key implementation**: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` 36→ 37→### Via `/erk:pr-submit` or `/erk:git-pr-push`
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.param:
The primary path for creating issue-PR linkages:
1. Creates branch from plan issue (e.g., `P123-feature-12-11-0948`)

The primary path for creating plan-PR linkages:
1. Creates branch from plan (e.g., `P123-feature-12-11-0948`)

4. Displays 🔗 indicator for PRs that will close the issue
**Display format**: `#123 🔗` indicates PR #123 will close the issue when merged.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Important behavior**: GitHub's `willCloseTarget` field is set at PR creation time only. ...[truncated 711 chars]...t` passes the "Closes" text to `create_pr()` rather than adding it via `update_pr_body()`.

**Important behavior**: GitHub's `willCloseTarget` field is set at PR creation time only. ...[truncated 814 chars]... GitHub uses "issue" terminology internally, erk refers to these as "plans" or "plan PRs".

Running `erk exec plan-update` ensures reviewers see changes in both locations. 121:| **File modified** | `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md` | `.erk/impl-context/plan.md` | 122:| **Sync mechanism** | Git push + plan sync to issue | Git push only (no issue sync) | 154:When the branch name follows the `plnd/` prefix pattern (e.g., `plnd/add-feature-03-07-1234`), the command automatically updates the plan issue with dispatch metadata:

docs/learned/architecture/phase-zero-detection-pattern.md:62:This is a distinct mode from Plan Review Mode — it targets a different file (`.erk/impl-context/plan.md` vs `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md`) and uses a simpler push workflow (no issue sync, no graphite submit). docs/learned/erk/pr-address-workflows.md:71:| **File modified** | Source code files | `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md` | docs/learned/erk/pr-address-workflows.md:79:2. **Edit plan file**: Make changes to `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md` based on feedback docs/learned/erk/pr-address-workflows.md:90:- **PR branch**: `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md` file (for version control) docs/learned/erk/pr-address-workflows.md:121:| **File modified** | `PLAN-REVIEW-{issue}.md` | `.erk/impl-context/plan.md` |

13: - action: "adding new exec script parameters with 'issue' in the name" 14: warning: "When adding new exec script parameters, use 'plan' terminology not 'issue'. See erk-exec-commands.md Phase 5 Terminology Standardization." 130:erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521 # From plan number 144:- `--issue` - Plan number to fetch plan from 178:erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521 # From plan number 211:These commands were renamed from issue-oriented to PR-oriented terminology: 215:| `close-issue-with-comment` | `close-pr` | 216:| `plan-update-issue` | `plan-update` | 217:| `setup-impl-from-issue` | `setup-impl-from-pr` | 218:| `issue-title-to-filename` | `plan-title-to-filename` | 219:| `create-issue-from-session` | `create-pr-from-session` | 223:CLI flags and error codes were standardized from "issue" to "plan" terminology: 227:| `--plan-issue` (flag) | `--learn-plan` | 228:| `missing-plan-issue` (error code) | `missing-learn-plan` | 229:| `unexpected-plan-issue` (error code) | `unexpected-learn-plan` | 230:| `no-issue-reference` (error code) | `no-plan-reference` | 231:| `issue-not-found` (error code) | `plan-not-found` |

76:The "Closes" keyword (or equivalent: "Fixes", "Resolves") in the PR body triggers GitHub's issue auto-close behavior. See [GitHub's documentation on linking keywords](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/working-with-advanced-formatting/using-keywords-in-issues-and-pull-requests). 82:GitHub stores cross-reference events on the issue timeline. These are what erk queries to display linkages in `erk pr list`.
No matches found

37:Step 1 (`prepare_state()`) resolves 6 fields: `repo_root`, `branch_name`, `parent_branch`, `trunk_branch`, `plan_id`, and validates `.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json` linkage (with legacy `.erk/impl-context/issue.json` fallback). 100:**When to remove this:** When no worktrees exist with `.erk/impl-context/` but missing a reference file, grep for `.erk/impl-context` directories and check for `plan-ref.json` or `issue.json` presence. If all have one, the auto-repair is dead code.
No matches found

- **erk-exec-commands.md**: Tripwire about `'issue'` in parameter names (meta-reference), `--issue` flag (actual CLI flag still in code), and historical renaming tables documenting what was changed - **pr-submit-pipeline.md**: `issue.json` references to actual legacy file names - **issue-pr-linkage-storage.md**: GitHub's own "issue" terminology for auto-close behavior and timeline events
Let me also verify markdown links aren't broken:
session.tool_result: 76:The "Closes" keyword (or equivalent: "Fixes", "Resolves") in the PR body triggers GitHub's issue auto-close behavior.
